Ring of the Sky  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FINGER
AC: 10
STA: +10 HP: +90
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Ring of the Sky
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FINGER
AC: 10
STA: +10 HP: +90
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Mavik
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-25 10:12:48
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Temple of Veeshan
NPC Name
Eashen of the Sky


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Temple of Veeshan

Ring of the Sky item window 2017

You know
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2003 at 3:06 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591771758308
View User Forum
Sage
74 posts
I am sick and tired of looking at items and to have the entire forum nothing but a waste of space with all the arguing over "what class" gets a particular item. ***** the arguing people, argue with your guildleaders over who gets what loot. These forums could be so much more productive if you posted........useful info about the mobs that drop these uber items. Damned monks anyway starting "looks like a monk iteem"
▲ Page top
RE: You know
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2003 at 2:21 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591759006590
Scholar
72 posts
Aren't most guilds that are in NToV doing DKP anyway? Arguing over who gets an item seems outdated. The person who gets the item is the one with the most DKP who is willing to spend some on the item. Period. DKP was invented with the intention of eliminating these noisy and pointless loot discussions.
▲ Page top
RE: You know
QuoteReply
# Mar 20 2003 at 3:11 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
It's amazing how a couple of retards and morons who probably aren't even monks can give an entire class community a bad name. Thanks.
▲ Page top
wars first
QuoteReply
# Jul 30 2002 at 3:08 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591759939836
121 posts
this ring basically comes out to be 10 ac ~160 hp...thus why it will go to warriors who need it first.
▲ Page top
RE: wars first
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2002 at 7:13 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966074874
View User Forum
25 posts
This item is generally not warrior first despite the arguments made that it should be. There are alot of classes that value hp above all at this stage in the game, not just warriors. Warriors, monks, shaman, paladins, shadowknights, necromancers, rogues, and rangers would all aspire for this item. There are quite a few rings out there now with alot of HP and Sta. I personally would not spend 16 raid credits on it for my shaman required in my guild.

Venerable Gazkan Drooidslayer
Oracle of Forged Souls
The Seventh Hammer
▲ Page top
RE: wars first
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2003 at 7:48 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592760100047
View User Forum
7 posts
Don't forget enchanters. Keeping your enchanters alive long enough to cast more runes or to get off the cripple could mean the success of a close encounter.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 27 2002 at 4:04 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ummm.... if your in a guild who can take down this guy this ring would go to one of the warriors in their main tank line...simple as that..
RE: Warrior ring
QuoteReply
# Aug 17 2003 at 6:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595780131429
464 posts
Not as simple as that, I'm afraid. We've never killed this guy and I'm maxed on STA. There are plenty of Warriors who wouldn't bother with this. I'm not gonna give up the resists on my ring for another 20 or so HPs. It's not worth it. The STA isn't worthwhile for me because with shammie buffs I'm over max already so it's wasted 95% of the time.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 30 2001 at 1:31 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If the leader of the raid doesn't give this item to the main tanking warrior in his guild, he doesn't have his head screwed on straight.
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 16 2002 at 6:20 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This ring sits nicely on my Monk :)
RE: Warrior item
QuoteReply
# Oct 10 2001 at 12:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767344930
4 posts
As a 60 rogue this gives me 6 ac and 65 hp over the 6/65 ring I used to wear. Hp which helps me stay alive longer dealing the damage I do.
but that's not a real reason why warriors may not get this ring. The real reason is that if your guild is killing Ntov dragons (or in this case psuedo-Ntov) there are ring better suited to warrior. Rings with 30ac rings with 10 ac and Stats and Resists. and for a warrior one of the hardest things to do is get decent resists without nuking his ac. an extra 90 hp simply won't make much a difference when he's being CH'd before his health ever drops below 3 bubbles.
Granted warriors should have high hit points, but more than that they should have high ac, and as high resists as they can manage, something this ring does not provide.
▲ Page top
RE: Warrior item
QuoteReply
# Jun 26 2002 at 2:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592246838066
28 posts
Zyke you have absolutely no clue what you're talking about. At the high lvl game for warriors HP's are EVERYTHING. AC is maxed very easily, Resists are high enough...it all comes down to HP. The best tanks in the best guilds have 100hp items in every slot...why....becauce HP are king. In the end game mobs are hitting for upwards of 1500 a shot..quadding hitting for that. Maximizing hp's is the only thing that makes these mobs possible.

Xisix Anklebane
60 Rogue of Kaizen'X
Emarr
▲ Page top
RE: Warrior item
QuoteReply
# May 01 2004 at 3:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592254072480
89 posts
haha you guys ever go back and read some posts from like 02 like this one where it says "In the end game mobs are hitting for upwards of 1500 a shot" its just so funny to see that to me :P now adays you WISH just yard trash was hitting for that haha. Its great I never got to tank anything in tov as a ranger when it was "uber" now i ***** slap the whole got damn place....change, is our...friend haha


Rangrr Grrl of TP
▲ Page top
RE: Warrior item
QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2002 at 2:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591774704467
63 posts
I agree with the thing of getting resists up without nuking AC, I am a 60 warrior and the secondary tank of my Guild. getting

I disagree with AC > HP- I rate both things equal, 1500ac doesnt help you much when you just have 5300 HP fully buffed.
ATM I am pretty much done with my gear, I got Full Kael quest armor but BP (Berserker) and no real newbie crap but Rings (6ac65hp) and earrings ( cougarclaw), weapons are epics (upgrading will be hard as warders are all dead, I dont want to upgrade anyways I love my epics)

Yes
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2001 at 4:48 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
yes, drops off Eashen of the Sky in ToV.


-Valero
60th Warlord of The Vagrants
▲ Page top
AGI=AC, STA=HP
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2001 at 1:04 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Jarnin, agility increases AC, stamina increases maximum hit points, and makes the yellow bar drop slower when swinging weapons which keeps the melee fighters from hitting like oom enchanters.
▲ Page top
RE: AGI=AC, STA=HP
QuoteReply
# May 04 2001 at 9:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756318483
13 posts
As long as you have 100 Stamina you'll be fine. The only effect that is noticable with oos ( out of Stamina ) is you will run slower. I have been in 10 minute long battles with mob bosses up in the PoG and was oos for 7+ minutes and I could tell no difference. I have also seen it writen too many times.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 24 2001 at 11:42 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Um, 10 AC. +5 stamina @ L50 = 10 AC.
RE: This ring has Monk written all over it...
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 10:20 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
ermm.....stamina doesn't make AC....sta makes hps...agi makes AC. So its more of a 10ac 105-110hp ring. And even if it were agi on it it woldn't work that way...it would use the base 10ac add that then add ac from agi...which would be maybe 2visible ac. but it is sta not agi on it.

so its a 10ac 105-110hp ring
▲ Page top
Incorrect
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 8:53 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592757352467
1 post
The way it actually works is it totals up all of your ac from items and multiplies by a modifier based on defense skill and agi.

Nazdul Bonechewer
53 Champion of Talionis
Prexus Server
▲ Page top
RE: This ring has Monk written all over it...

#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 24 2001 at 8:52 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Read carefully before you reply. I believe he was talking to the "oh-too" common blue appearance of the ring in inventory. Kinda like Lambent armor looks the same color as any other armor in inventory (silver), this ring looks just like any other light-blue ring.
